response: this belief is wrong and harmful it suggests that people with learning disabilities cannot succeed in school or achieve high levels of understanding it ignores the many ways in which these individuals can learn and grow it fails to recognize that learning disabilities do not define a person's potential it is important to understand that with the right support and resources people with learning disabilities can excel in academic areas and beyond this belief is based on a misunderstanding of what learning disabilities really mean it is a false idea that should be challenged and replaced with a more accurate and respectful view of all individuals regardless of their learning abilities
